phase: 1 of 9 complete; Phase 2 blocked on open question §4.2
nextAgent: human review of Phase 1, then Phase 2 (needs §4.2 decision first)

artifacts.qaTests: — (Phase 1 is pure data/logic; unit tests co-located in src/utils/routineEngine/ per .claude/rules/testing.md)

baseline.fullRun: npx jest src tests -> 1089 passed / 3 failed; the 3 are PRE-EXISTING (verified by stashing Phase 1 and re-running clean HEAD, which fails identically): tests/catalog/catalog-screen (AsyncStorage native module null), tests/catalog/product-detail (palette.cobaltTint undefined), tests/shelf-filtering/PaoChip.
baseline.note: Phase 2+ must not reduce this. Changed expectations must be justified in the progress log per architecture-review.md §6.

process.mode: inline — all four roles, no subagents (user directive 2026-07-17, applies to phases 1-9)
process.review: tech-lead replaced by a structured self-review pass, run SEPARATELY after implementation, judged strictly against artifacts (corrected phase file, discrepancy report, .claude/rules/architecture-review.md, the diff). A decision not written down is documented or flagged as a deviation. Output = pass/fail checklist with file/line refs; any FAIL is fixed and the checklist re-run.
reviewChecklist: single conflict matrix (pairRules), old conflictRulesDb collapsed
isStrongActive <=> irritancy >= 3 invariant, no exemption lists
cumulative active cap + rinseOff exemption implemented as specified
migrations idempotent (double-run test exists and passes)
every acceptance criterion in the corrected phase file mapped to a passing test
npx tsc --noEmit clean; no Math.random / unsorted object iteration in engine code

handoffNotes: PHASE 1 IS COMPLETE AND SELF-REVIEWED (ACCEPT), uncommitted on engine-improvements. Shipped: irritancy recalibration + irritancyByPotency on retinoid; resolveIrritancy/isStrongActive in rulesetTypes.ts as the single mild/strong boundary; peptide_signal + peptide_neuro with a conservative unknown-peptide fallback; conflict matrix consolidated onto actives.json pairRules (INGREDIENT_CONFLICT_RULES deleted, rule_vitc_niacinamide retired, 2 rules added); rinseOff on ProductFacts; ruleset version -> 2026-07-17.

PHASE 1 DEVIATION CARRIED INTO PHASE 3: glycerin_class was NOT added. Glycerin/glycols are in most formulations, so attributing by bare INCI presence tagged nearly every product and would flatten Phase 3 goal scoring (every product would hit the dryness concern). It has no Phase 1 consumer. Phase 3 adds it WITH the goals block and must pick a real attribution rule — INCI position (lists are concentration-ordered) or productType, not bare presence. This is a clinical-consultant data question, same review as the goals values.

PHASE 1 DEVIATION ALREADY APPLIED (was scheduled for Phase 4): pha lost its stacking block AND left aha/bha sharedCapWith. The Phase 1 invariant (stacking iff irritancy>=3) fails on pha at irritancy 1. Both edits are inseparable: leaving pha in the shared group makes the cap admission-order dependent (PHA-first blocks a later AHA; AHA-first does not) — a determinism defect. AHA + PHA in one PM is now legal, as report §7.2 predicted; exfoliant exposure is still capped by phototype_pih_exfoliant_cap and summer_uv_exfoliant_limit.

PHASE 4 REMINDER: assumption 8.1 still holds — the cumulative cap subsumes per-class stacking, so Phase 4 deletes the remaining stacking blocks (including the vitamin_c_pure one Phase 1 just added, which is the short-lived churn 8.1 predicted) and restates the invariant as "subject to the cumulative cap iff irritancy >= 3". rinseOff is already shipped and waiting for its Phase 4 consumer.

PHASE 7 REMINDER (sharpens §4.4): reasonCode and ruleId are conflated at resolve.ts:410 (`reasonCode: primary.ruleId`), so FrozenItem.reasonCode currently holds pair-rule IDs. Phase 1 changed that id set (rule_vitc_niacinamide deleted, 2 added), which is exactly why the enum must not absorb rule IDs — add a reasonCode field to pairRules and split the two vocabularies first.

New shared primitive: conflictEngine.ts exports matchPairRule(keyA, keyB) — the ONE place a class pair is matched against pairRules. src/utils/productForm/conflictPreview.ts (a third legacy-table consumer the tech design missed) now calls it too, so the preview and the real check cannot disagree. Reuse it rather than re-implementing side matching; pairRules sides may be arrays.

READ DISCREPANCY-REPORT.md BEFORE ANY PHASE. The V2.1 package was authored against the V2 design document, not the merged V2 implementation. 4 of 9 phases rested on problems that do not exist. A session that trusts the original prose will 'fix' non-bugs and fork the taxonomy.

Already exist — do not build: the irritancy scale (actives.json properties.irritancy, all 15 classes); PERIOD_ELIGIBILITY (= allowedPeriods + preferredPeriod, matches the spec 1:1); the Path A period guard (resolve.ts:420 — retinoid-in-AM already unreachable); the planContainsProperty:photosensitizing SPF mechanism; a pure seasonMask (engine takes it as EngineInput); the phototype + vitamin C migrations (schema v2); recency-free scoring.
Do not exist despite the spec naming them: PEPT tag, maxActivesPerPeriod, skinIssues (it is concerns: SkinConcern[]), assets/inci_seed.json, InciRepository, anything pregnancy-related.

src/constants/rulesets/actives.json is the single source of truth. activeRegistry.ts and conflictMatrix.ts are NOT created — folded in. A third conflict table is a tech-lead BLOCKER under architecture-review.md §3.

Conventions that override the package's prose: class keys keep persisted names (copper_peptides, ceramides, cica, hyaluronic_acid, spf_filters — never peptide_copper); severity is avoid/caution (never high/medium); reason codes are lower_snake_case (never SCREAMING_CASE).

PHASE 1 SCOPE: irritancy recalibration (bha 2->3, vitamin_c_pure 2->3, benzoyl_peroxide 3->4, azelaic_acid 1->2, copper_peptides 1->2); irritancyByPotency on retinoid (low/medium 3, high/rx 4) resolved in productFacts.aggregateProperties; isStrongActive = irritancy >= 3 derivation + rulesetIntegrity invariant (stacking declared iff strong -> vitamin_c_pure gains stacking); peptide_signal / peptide_neuro / glycerin_class classes + matchers + the contains-'peptide' fallback to peptide_signal; pairRules consolidation (delete conflictRulesDb INGREDIENT_CONFLICT_RULES, repoint ConflictEngine, remove rule_vitc_niacinamide, add vitc_pure+copper_peptides and vitc_derivative+bpo); rinseOff on ProductFacts.

PHASE 1 NON-SCOPE: the cumulative cap is ENFORCED in Phase 4, not here. rinseOff is unused until Phase 4 — that is intended, not dead code. No cumulative_active_cap emission in Phase 1. pregnancyBlocked class property is NOT added (deferred with report §4.3).
OPEN QUESTION §4.1 RESOLVED (user, 2026-07-17): vitamin_c_pure -> irritancy 3, cap intended. Superseded in scope by the cumulative active exposure rule (report §7).

OPEN QUESTIONS STILL BLOCKING LATER PHASES: §4.2 pm_preferred for AHA/BHA loosens a shipped PM-only rule (blocks Phase 2; recommend dropping); §4.3 pregnancy is a whole subsystem not a flag (blocks Phase 3; recommend splitting out); §4.4 the closed DecisionReasonCode enum must absorb 22 existing JSON codes + a synthesized stacking_cap_* family, AND resolve.ts:410 writes reasonCode: primary.ruleId so pair-rule IDs leak into the reason vocabulary — the enum cannot close until reasonCode and ruleId are split (blocks Phase 7).

CUMULATIVE ACTIVE EXPOSURE RULE (user directive 2026-07-17, report §7) amends Phases 1 and 4. An active class is a property of a product, not a slot. Mild (irritancy <= 2): no cumulative restriction, any number, any slot; class-dedup applies ONLY to treatment-candidate selection. Strong (irritancy >= 3) leave-on: one carrier per period across ALL slots; reclassified as a treatment candidate in any format (acid toner/cream = treatments), inheriting treatment frequency caps and never a structural slot's daily frequency; the vacated structural slot takes a neutral alternative or renders a placeholder. Rinse-off carriers are exempt with an info-level note. Reason code cumulative_active_cap.

Four sub-questions the directive does not state, resolved as DOCUMENTED ASSUMPTIONS (report §7.1-§7.4), not silently: (8.1) the cumulative cap subsumes per-class stacking — Phase 1 adds stacking to vitamin_c_pure to keep the shipped invariant coherent, Phase 4 deletes all stacking blocks; deliberate short-lived churn. (8.2) AHA + PHA in one period becomes LEGAL — PHA is mild so it leaves aha.sharedCapWith; a loosening required by the mild/strong split; if unintended, PHA needs irritancy 3 or an explicit exfoliant-family cap. (8.3) 'treatment frequency caps' resolve per-class — exfoliant cap for exfoliants, existing adaptation phase caps for retinoids; no new table; enforced invariant is the narrow 'never daily'. (8.4) rinseOff true for cleanser + makeup_remover only; peeling and mask are conservatively leave-on since peel gels rinse but pads do not and sleeping masks are leave-on.

PHASE 5 CARRIES A DOCUMENTED-DECISION REVERSAL: adaptation.ts:31-35 states 'A product owned long before tracking shipped lands directly in phase 3 (no retroactive throttling)' as deliberate design. The usage-anchor intent requires the opposite. The implementer must update that JSDoc and log the reversal, or architecture-review.md §6 auto-blocks.

PHASE 5 CONSTRAINT: generatePlan is pure and never writes (generate.ts:39). firstScheduledDate must be recorded on the save path (src/domain/routinePlanActions.ts) and threaded back in via TrackingInput — do not make the engine write.
Phase 8 depends on 1 AND 3 (it executes the goal migration). Only ONE schema bump for the whole package: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 2 -> 3.
